steps.title: Interpret the function structure
steps.content:
The function $f(n) = 42 - 6n$ represents the remaining balance after renting $n$ games.

This follows the general pattern:
remaining balance = initial amount − (cost per game) × n
Here, the \$42 is the initial amount, and the coefficient of $n$ tells us how the balance changes per game.

steps.title: Determine the cost per game
steps.content:
In $f(n) = 42 - 6n$, the coefficient of $n$ is $-6$. The negative value indicates the balance decreases; the magnitude tells us the decrease per game.

The balance decreases by \$6 for each game rented, so each rental costs \$6. B is correct.
